gates.title: Packaged update still leaves the runtime ready without an immediate doctor repair step
evidence_required: Packaged update syncs runtime cron definitions after npm bump
Packaged update reloads managed macOS LaunchAgents after a real version change
Same-file hook sync noise no longer pollutes the packaged update path
Runtime doctor no longer keeps cleaned packaged installs red just because recent Codex transcript drift is already tracked with zero open conditioned debt

gates.title: Packaged migration carries newly added root runtime modules into ~/.nexo on upgraded installs
evidence_required: The packaged installer discovers top-level runtime Python modules dynamically instead of relying only on a manual allowlist
Real-machine validation confirms newly shipped commands such as nexo export / nexo import work after nexo update on an existing packaged install
Regression coverage fails if future releases stop discovering new root runtime modules

gates.title: Package, docs, plugin artifacts, and website tell the same v5.3.8 story
evidence_required: Root package and integration artifacts all point to 5.3.8
README, changelog, llms, and release contract mention the packaged migration hotfix clearly
Website home, blog, changelog, llms, and sitemap are updated to 5.3.8
